title: The Shift from  the Modi Govt’s Earlier “Muscular Foreign Policy” to the Current Conciliatory Neighbourhood  Policy is Noticeable in  India’s Response Towards Non-electoral Changes at the top in Myanmar, Nepal, Afghanistan, Pakistan and Sri Lanka.
body_lines: In late 2016, a senior Indian government official, in a meeting with the envoy of a neighbouring country, spelt out the consequences that the country would face if it crossed the Narendra Modi government’s newly “muscular foreign policy”. At the time, the Government had put Pakistan on notice on terror attacks, and cancelled Foreign Secretary-level talks; in Sri Lanka, the Rajapaksa government, that had been seen cozying up to China was voted out, with some reports that Indian intelligence played a role in facilitating opposition talks; and in Nepal, Prime Minister K.P. Sharma Oli’s coalition government had fallen apart, with a similar nudge reported from New Delhi.

The message the official conveyed was that New Delhi would use all its levers to prevail over any uncooperative South Asian neighbour. However, the tough messaging did not find much favour over time. By the Modi government’s second term, it had made peace with a much more consensual, conciliatory policy in the neighbourhood — visibly improving ties with each country (minus Pakistan) through high-level visits, extending development aid and lines of credit, and enabling a rush of soft power diplomacy.

The contrast between India’s response to the events in 2016 to the present is stark, after five neighbouring democracies underwent non-electoral changes at the top, namely Myanmar, Nepal, Afghanistan, Pakistan and Sri Lanka. The first difference is that New Delhi has not been held responsible in any of its neighbouring capitals for attempting to interfere in their political processes. Second, South Block has abandoned its uniformly muscular “one size fits all” approach to the region.

While in Myanmar, the Modi government continued engagement and even strengthened ties with the military junta that overthrew the government led by Aung San Suu Kyi’s National League for Democracy, in Afghanistan it severed ties with the Taliban that took power in Kabul by force after Afghan President Ashraf Ghani left the country. In Nepal where Prime Minister Sher Bahadur Deuba came to power after the Supreme Court dismissed K.P. Oli, and Sri Lanka, where public protests forced Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa to resign and President Gotabaya Rajapaksa to appoint rival and Opposition leader Ranil Wickremesinghe as the new Prime Minister, New Delhi has been largely supportive of the processes. Whereas in Pakistan, it has virtually ignored the swearing-in of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif after Imran Khan lost the vote of confidence.

A second lesson that seems to have been learnt is that New Delhi’s messaging now is focused on people in the neighbourhood rather than just those in power. In Afghanistan, for example, the Modi government spent months in careful negotiation with Pakistani officials to ensure it could send 50,000 MT of wheat meant for the Afghan people, despite the fact that it has no diplomatic engagement with either Islamabad or Kabul otherwise. In Sri Lanka, a Ministry of External Affairs statement said that India would “always be guided by the best interests of the people of Sri Lanka expressed through democratic processes”, a subtle pitch both to the people and to democratic processes in the region. A third lesson is perhaps the toning down of rhetoric on domestic issues in the neighbourhood — the Government’s public reaction to Durga Pooja violence against Bangladesh’s Hindu minority last year was much more nuanced than its messaging during the push ahead for the Citizenship (Amendment) Act in 2019. Some have even suggested that the continued delay in framing rules for the CAA since then has more to do with concern for ties with Dhaka than with the COVID-19 pandemic.

There are, however, other lessons that New Delhi must learn from the regime changes in the neighbourhood, and some of them apply to the Indian context as well. This is after all, the Indian subcontinent, set in the Indian Ocean, and what happens here cannot leave India untouched. Therefore, a silent or “neutral” position cannot mark the Modi government’s response to the changes in the way it has with Russia’s invasion of Ukraine or China’s moves in Hong Kong or the South China Sea region. India faces the direct impact of almost every South Asian country in crisis, in terms of the need for aid and loans or a possible influx of refugees, as movements that develop in one neighbouring country are often mirrored in another. Therefore, they must be watched more closely.

The first lesson to be learnt is that populism does not pay in the long run. While the potent combination of hyper-nationalism, religious majoritarianism, and a strident anti-elitism can bring “men of the masses” such as Mahinda Rajapaksa, K.P. Oli, and Imran Khan to power (as they promise an alternative to corrupt, dynasti regimes), it does not necessarily keep them there. It is a mistake for any government to conflate an electoral win and a mandate for governance with a carte blanche for ruling a country.

The second is that the popularity of a leader can decline sharply and suddenly for one or a combination of reasons: K.P. Oli won a landslide victory in 2017 where his Left Alliance secured majorities in both houses, and formed governments in six of seven provinces; Imran Khan won all five National Assembly seats he fought in the 2018 elections, and while his party did not win a majority of seats, it won the popular vote; and the Rajapaksa-led Sri Lanka People’s Party (SLPP) ruling coalition won 150 of 225 parliamentary seats in 2020. That these popular mandates could be cast aside in just a few years is a stark reminder that nothing is forever, especially in a democracy.

It is also clear that during the crises that Nepal, Pakistan and Sri Lanka faced, the leaders who stepped into the breach may not have had the same political prowess or oratory presence as the leaders they replaced, but were acceptable both domestically and internationally because they had experience and education on their side. Mr. Deuba became Prime Minister for the fifth time, for example, Mr. Sharif had the longest tenure as Chief Minister of Pakistan’s Punjab in three terms before he became Prime Minister, and Ranil Wickremesinghe was appointed Prime Minister for the sixth time.

The next lesson is one that United States President George H.W. Bush learned in 1992 despite the Iraq war and his pitch to patriotism, as Bill Clinton defeated him in an election where the big slogan was “It’s the economy, stupid”. In Pakistan, Nepal and Sri Lanka, the defeat of populists has come not so much at the hand of the Opposition parties, but by the slowdown in growth, jobs and rising inflation. India had already seen six successive quarters of straight losses in December 2019, and most of the neighbourhood was floundering as well, when COVID-19 was first reported.

In the years that followed, the COVID-19 pandemic enforced lockdowns, and the resultant slowdown in the global economy made GDP figures in the region plummet. More recently, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and sanctions by the West have made food and fuel prices soar. In such a situation, the change in power in these three countries took only a small push, from the military, the courts, or from street protests. New Delhi must not only study the causes of the economic mismanagement that brought change in the neighbourhood but must also survey the impact of new vulnerabilities on smaller neighbouring countries that could be exploited by global powers as they seek a more direct influence in the region.

Given the common challenges the region faces, New Delhi must find newer ways to energise regional groupings such as the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) and the Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal (BBIN) Initiative, and even to reconsider SAARC, in order to discuss shared approaches to reviving tourism and exports, supporting South Asian expatriate labour abroad, and building common pools of food and fuel stocks to soften inflationary blows on the South Asian economy.

Finally, the Modi government must learn from the lessons in political culture that let down the “alpha leaders” in neighbouring democracies. One of the common threads in each of the governments (Rajapaksa, Oli and Khan) was an abhorrence for consensus building. In various ways, each of them turned their opposition into “the enemy”, and froze out the media, non-governmental organisations, and any voting constituency other than their own.

Nations, especially democracies run on many engines — not just the single monolithic one of the party or people in power. As New Delhi essays its role as a regional leader, the Government would be wise to not only study the impact of changes in the neighbourhood on Indian policy but also to look into the mirror the neighbours hold up to India, for a better understanding of its future challenges within the country.

si.title: මෝදි රජයේ පෙර "මාංශපේශී විදේශ ප්‍රතිපත්තිය" සිට වර්තමාන සහනශීලී අසල්වැසි ප්‍රතිපත්තිය වෙත මාරුවීම, මියන්මාරය, නේපාලය, ඇෆ්ගනිස්ථානය, පකිස්ථානය සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව යන රටවල ඉහලින්ම ඇති මැතිවරණ නොවන වෙනස්කම් සඳහා ඉන්දියාවේ ප්‍රතිචාරය තුළ කැපී පෙනේ.
body_lines: 2016 අගභාගයේදී, ඉන්දීය රජයේ ජ්‍යෙෂ්ඨ නිලධාරියෙක්, අසල්වැසි රටක නියෝජිතයෙකු සමඟ පැවති සාකච්ඡාවකදී, නරේන්ද්‍ර මෝදි ආන්ඩුවේ අලුතින් ගෙන ආ "මාංශපේශී විදේශ ප්‍රතිපත්තිය" ඉක්මවා ගියහොත් රට මුහුණ දෙන ප්‍රතිවිපාක විස්තර කළේය. එකල රජය ත්‍රස්ත ප්‍රහාර සම්බන්ධයෙන් පාකිස්තානයට දැනුම් දී තිබූ අතර විදේශ ලේකම් මට්ටමේ සාකච්ඡා අවලංගු කර තිබුණි. ශ‍්‍රී ලංකාව තුළ, චීනය සමඟ සුහදව කටයුතු කරන බව දුටු රාජපක්ෂ රජය ඡන්දය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කරන ලද අතර, විපක්ෂ සාකච්ඡා සඳහා පහසුකම් සැලසීම සඳහා ඉන්දීය බුද්ධි අංශ කාර්යභාරයක් ඉටු කළ බවට ඇතැම් වාර්තා පළ විය. සහ නේපාලයේ අගමැති කේ.පී. ශර්මා ඔලිගේ සභාග ආන්ඩුව ද බිඳ වැටී ඇති අතර, නව දිල්ලියෙන් ද එවැනිම නෝක්කාඩුවක් වාර්තා විය.

නිළධාරියා විසින් එවන ලද පණිවිඩය වූයේ, නව දිල්ලිය ඕනෑම සහයෝගීතාවයකින් තොර දකුණු ආසියාතික අසල්වැසියෙකු අභිබවා යාමට සිය සියලු ලීවර භාවිතා කරන බවයි. කෙසේ වෙතත්, කාලයත් සමඟ දැඩි පණිවිඩ යැවීමට එතරම් ප්‍රසාදයක් නොලැබුණි. මෝඩි ආන්ඩුවේ දෙවන ධූර කාලය වන විට, එය අසල්වැසි ප්‍රදේශයේ වඩාත් සම්මුතිවාදී, සහනශීලී ප්‍රතිපත්තියක් සමඟ සාමය ඇති කර ගෙන ඇත - ඉහළ මට්ටමේ සංචාර හරහා එක් එක් රටවල් (පකිස්ථානයෙන් අඩු) දෘශ්‍යමාන ලෙස සබඳතා වැඩිදියුණු කිරීම, සංවර්ධන ආධාර සහ ණය මාර්ග දිගු කිරීම සහ සක්‍රීය කිරීම මෘදු බල රාජ්‍ය තාන්ත්‍රික කඩිමුඩියේ.

මියන්මාරය, නේපාලය, ඇෆ්ගනිස්ථානය, පකිස්ථානය සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව යන අසල්වැසි ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී රටවල් පහක් ඉහළින් මැතිවරණ නොවන වෙනස්කම්වලට භාජනය වීමෙන් පසුව, 2016 සිදුවීම් සම්බන්ධයෙන් ඉන්දියාවේ ප්‍රතිචාරය අතර වර්තමානය දක්වා ඇති වෙනස කැපී පෙනේ. පළමු වෙනස නම්, ඔවුන්ගේ දේශපාලන ක්‍රියාවලීන්ට මැදිහත් වීමට උත්සාහ කිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් නව දිල්ලිය එහි අසල්වැසි අගනුවර කිසිවකුට වගකිව යුතු නොවීමයි. දෙවනුව, සවුත් බ්ලොක් කලාපයට එහි ඒකාකාර මාංශපේශී "සියල්ලට ගැලපෙන එක ප්‍රමාණය" ප්‍රවේශය අතහැර දමා ඇත.

මියන්මාරයේ සිටියදී, මෝඩි ආන්ඩුව, අවුන්සාන් සුකීගේ ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදය සඳහා වූ ජාතික ලීගය විසින් නායකත්වය දුන් ආන්ඩුව පෙරලා දැමූ මිලිටරි ජුන්ටාව සමඟ සබඳතා දිගටම කරගෙන ගිය අතර, ඇෆ්ගනිස්ථානයේ දී එය ඇෆ්ගන් ජනාධිපතිවරයාගෙන් පසුව බලහත්කාරයෙන් කාබුල්හි බලය අල්ලා ගත් තලේබාන් සංවිධානය සමඟ සබඳතා බිඳ දැමීය. අෂ්රෆ් ගානි රට ගියා. නේපාලයේ අගමැති Sher Bahadur Deuba බලයට පත්වූයේ ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය විසින් K.P. ඔලි, සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව, මහජන විරෝධතා හේතුවෙන් අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂට ඉල්ලා අස්වීමට බල කළ අතර ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂට ප්‍රතිවාදී සහ විපක්ෂ නායක රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ නව අගමැති ලෙස පත් කිරීමට සිදු වූ විට, නවදිල්ලිය බොහෝ දුරට ක්‍රියාවලීන්ට සහාය වී ඇත. පකිස්ථානයේ දී, ඉම්රාන් ඛාන් විශ්වාසභංගයෙන් පරාජයට පත්වීමෙන් පසු අගමැති ෂෙබාස් ෂරීෆ්ගේ දිවුරුම් දීම පාහේ නොසලකා හැර තිබේ.

ඉගෙන ගෙන ඇති බව පෙනෙන දෙවන පාඩම නම්, නව දිල්ලියේ පණිවිඩ යැවීම දැන් බලයේ සිටින අයට වඩා අසල්වැසි පුද්ගලයින් කෙරෙහි අවධානය යොමු කර ඇති බවයි. නිදසුනක් වශයෙන්, ඇෆ්ගනිස්ථානයේ, ඉස්ලාමාබාද් හෝ කාබුල් සමඟ රාජ්‍ය තාන්ත්‍රික සම්බන්ධයක් නොමැති බව නොතකා, ඇෆ්ගනිස්ථානයේ ජනතාව සඳහා තිරිඟු මෙට්‍රික් ටොන් 50,000ක් එවිය හැකි බව සහතික කිරීම සඳහා මෝඩි ආන්ඩුව පකිස්තාන නිලධාරීන් සමඟ මාස ගණනාවක් ප්‍රවේශමෙන් සාකච්ඡා කළේය. ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ, විදේශ කටයුතු අමාත්‍යාංශයේ ප්‍රකාශයක් කියා සිටියේ, ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී ක්‍රියාවලීන් තුළින් ප්‍රකාශිත ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ජනතාවගේ යහපත වෙනුවෙන් ඉන්දියාව සැමවිටම මඟ පෙන්වනු ඇති බවයි, එය ජනතාවට සහ කලාපයේ ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී ක්‍රියාවලීන්ට සියුම් තණතීරුවකි. තුන්වන පාඩම නම් අසල්වැසි ප්‍රදේශවල ගෘහස්ථ ප්‍රශ්න පිළිබඳ වාචාල කතා අඩු කිරීම විය හැකිය - පසුගිය වසරේ බංග්ලාදේශයේ හින්දු සුළුතරයට එරෙහිව දුර්ගා පූජා ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වයට රජය දැක්වූ මහජන ප්‍රතිචාරය, පුරවැසි (සංශෝධන) පනත සඳහා ඉදිරියට ගෙන යන තල්ලුව අතරතුර එහි පණිවිඩ යැවීමට වඩා බොහෝ සූක්ෂ්ම විය. 2019. එතැන් සිට CAA සඳහා නීති රීති සැකසීමේ අඛණ්ඩ ප්‍රමාදය COVID-19 වසංගතයට වඩා ඩකා සමඟ ඇති සබඳතා ගැන සැලකිලිමත් වන බව සමහරු යෝජනා කර ඇත.

කෙසේ වෙතත්, අසල්වැසි පාලන තන්ත්‍ර වෙනස්වීම් වලින් නවදිල්ලිය විසින් උගත යුතු වෙනත් පාඩම් ඇති අතර ඒවායින් සමහරක් ඉන්දියානු සන්දර්භයට ද අදාළ වේ. මේ සියල්ලට පසු, ඉන්දියානු සාගරයේ පිහිටුවා ඇති ඉන්දියානු උපමහාද්වීපය, සහ මෙහි සිදුවන දේ ඉන්දියාවට අත නොතැබිය නොහැක. එබැවින්, රුසියාව යුක්රේනය ආක්‍රමණය කිරීම හෝ හොංකොං හෝ දකුනු චීන මුහුදු කලාපයේ චීනයේ පියවර සමඟ ඇති වෙනස්වීම් වලට මෝදි ආන්ඩුව දක්වන ප්‍රතිචාරය නිශ්ශබ්ද හෝ "මධ්‍යස්ථ" ආස්ථානයකට සලකුණු කළ නොහැක. එක් අසල්වැසි රටක වර්ධනය වන චලනයන් බොහෝ විට තවත් රටක පිළිබිඹු වන බැවින්, ආධාර සහ ණය අවශ්‍යතා හෝ සරණාගතයින්ගේ ගලා ඒම සම්බන්ධයෙන්, අර්බුදයේ සිටින සෑම දකුණු ආසියානු රටකම පාහේ සෘජු බලපෑමට ඉන්දියාව මුහුණ දෙයි. එමනිසා, ඔවුන් වඩාත් සමීපව නිරීක්ෂණය කළ යුතුය.

උගත යුතු පළමු පාඩම වන්නේ ජනතාවාදය දිගුකාලීනව ගෙවන්නේ නැති බවයි. අධි-ජාතිකවාදය, ආගමික බහුතරවාදය සහ දැඩි ප්‍රභූ විරෝධයක ප්‍රබල සංකලනය මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ වැනි "ජනතාවගේ මිනිසුන්" ගෙන ආ හැකි අතර, කේ.පී. ඔලි, සහ ඉම්රාන් ඛාන් බලයට පත්වීම (ඔවුන් දූෂිත, රාජවංශ පාලන තන්ත්‍ර සඳහා විකල්පයක් පොරොන්දු වන පරිදි), එය ඔවුන්ව එහි තබා ගැනීම අවශ්‍ය නොවේ. ඕනෑම ආණ්ඩුවක් මැතිවරණ ජයග්‍රහණයක් සහ පාලනයක් සඳහා ජනවරමක් රටක් පාලනය කිරීම සඳහා කරත්තයක් සමඟ පටලවා ගැනීම වරදකි.

දෙවැන්න නම්, නායකයෙකුගේ ජනප්‍රියත්වය තියුනු ලෙස හා හදිසියේ පහත වැටීමට හේතු විය හැක්කේ එක් හේතුවක් නිසා ය: කේ.පී. ඔලි 2017 දී අතිවිශිෂ්ට ජයග්‍රහණයක් ලබා ගත් අතර එහිදී ඔහුගේ වාම සන්ධානය සභා දෙකේම බහුතර බලය ලබා ගත් අතර පළාත් හතෙන් හයකින් ආණ්ඩු පිහිටුවීය. ඉම්රාන් ඛාන් 2018 මැතිවරනයේදී ඔහු තරඟ කළ ජාතික සභා ආසන පහම ජයග්‍රහණය කළ අතර, ඔහුගේ පක්ෂය බහුතර ආසන සංඛ්‍යාවක් දිනා නොගත්තද, එය මහජන ඡන්දය දිනා ගත්තේය; සහ රාජපක්ෂ ප්‍රමුඛ ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහජන පක්ෂය (SLPP) පාලක හවුල 2020 දී පාර්ලිමේන්තු ආසන 225 න් 150 ක් දිනා ගත්තේය. මෙම ජනප්‍රිය ජනවරම වසර කිහිපයකින් පසෙකට දැමිය හැකි බව, විශේෂයෙන් ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී රටක කිසිවක් සදාකාලික නොවන බව සිහිගන්වන්නකි.

නේපාලය, පකිස්තානය සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව මුහුණ දුන් අර්බුද වලදී, කඩ කළ නායකයින්ට ඔවුන් වෙනුවට ආදේශ කළ නායකයින්ට සමාන දේශපාලන දක්ෂතාවයක් හෝ කථික පැවැත්මක් නොතිබිය හැකි නමුත් දේශීය හා ජාත්‍යන්තර වශයෙන් ඔවුන් පිළිගත හැකි බව පැහැදිලිය. ඔවුන්ගේ පැත්තෙන් අත්දැකීම් සහ අධ්‍යාපනය තිබුණා. Deuba මහතා පස්වන වරටත් අගමැති විය, උදාහරණයක් ලෙස, ෂරීෆ් මහතා අගමැති වීමට පෙර වාර තුනකදී පකිස්තානයේ පන්ජාබ් ප්‍රාන්තයේ මහ ඇමතිවරයා ලෙස දීර්ඝතම ධුර කාලය දැරූ අතර, රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා හයවන වරටත් අගමැති ලෙස පත් විය.

ඊළඟ පාඩම වන්නේ එක්සත් ජනපද ජනාධිපති ජෝර්ජ් එච්. බුෂ් 1992 දී ඉරාක යුද්ධය සහ දේශප්‍රේමය සඳහා ඔහුගේ තණතීරුව නොතකා, "එය ආර්ථිකය, මෝඩ" යන විශාල සටන් පාඨය වූ මැතිවරණයකදී බිල් ක්ලින්ටන් ඔහුව පරාජය කළ නිසා ඉගෙන ගත්තේය. පකිස්ථානයේ, නේපාලයේ සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ, ජනතාවාදීන්ගේ පරාජය පැමිණ ඇත්තේ විරුද්ධ පක්ෂ අතින් නොව, වර්ධනයේ මන්දගාමිත්වය, රැකියා සහ උද්ධමනය වැඩිවීම මගිනි. ඉන්දියාව දැනටමත් 2019 දෙසැම්බරයේ දී අඛණ්ඩව පාඩු ලැබූ කාර්තු හයක් දැක ඇති අතර, COVID-19 ප්‍රථම වරට වාර්තා වූ විට අසල්වැසි බොහෝ ප්‍රදේශ ද කලබල විය.

ඊළඟ වසරවලදී, COVID-19 වසංගතය අගුලු දැමීම් බලාත්මක කළ අතර, එහි ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස ගෝලීය ආර්ථිකයේ මන්දගාමිත්වය කලාපයේ දළ දේශීය නිෂ්පාදිතයේ අගයන් පහත වැටුණි. වඩාත් මෑතක දී, රුසියාව විසින් යුක්රේනය ආක්‍රමණය කිරීම සහ බටහිර විසින් සම්බාධක පැනවීම ආහාර හා ඉන්ධන මිල ඉහළ යාමට හේතු විය. එවැනි තත්වයක් තුළ, මෙම රටවල් තුනේ බලය වෙනස් කිරීම සඳහා හමුදාවෙන්, උසාවියෙන් හෝ වීදි උද්ඝෝෂණවලින් සිදු වූයේ කුඩා තල්ලුවක් පමණි. නව දිල්ලිය විසින් අසල්වැසි ප්‍රදේශයේ වෙනසක් ඇති කළ ආර්ථික වැරදි කළමනාකරණයේ හේතු අධ්‍යයනය කිරීම පමණක් නොව, කලාපය තුළ වඩාත් සෘජු බලපෑමක් අපේක්ෂා කරන විට ගෝලීය බලවතුන් විසින් සූරාකෑමට ලක්විය හැකි කුඩා අසල්වැසි රටවලට නව අවදානම් වල බලපෑම ද සමීක්ෂණය කළ යුතුය.

කලාපය මුහුණ දෙන පොදු අභියෝග සැලකිල්ලට ගෙන, නව දිල්ලිය බහු-ආංශික තාක්ෂණික සහ ආර්ථික සහයෝගීතාව සඳහා බෙංගාල බොක්ක මුල පිරීම (BIMSTEC) සහ බංග්ලාදේශය, භූතානය, ඉන්දියාව, නේපාලය (BBIN) මුලපිරීම වැනි කලාපීය කණ්ඩායම්වලට නව ක්‍රම සෙවිය යුතුය. සහ SAARC නැවත සලකා බැලීමට පවා, සංචාරක ව්‍යාපාරය සහ අපනයන පුනර්ජීවනය සඳහා හවුල් ප්‍රවේශයන් සාකච්ඡා කිරීම සඳහා, දකුණු ආසියාතික විදේශගත ශ්‍රමිකයින්ට විදේශයන්හි සහාය වීම සහ දකුණු ආසියානු ආර්ථිකය මත උද්ධමනකාරී පහරවල් ලිහිල් කිරීම සඳහා පොදු ආහාර සහ ඉන්ධන තොග ගොඩනැගීම.

අවසාන වශයෙන්, මෝඩි ආන්ඩුව අසල්වැසි ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී රටවල "ඇල්ෆා නායකයින්" පහත හෙලන දේශපාලන සංස්කෘතියේ පාඩම් වලින් උගත යුතුය. සෑම රජයකම (රාජපක්ෂ, ඔලි සහ ඛාන්) පොදු ත්‍රෙඩ් එකක් වූයේ සම්මුතිය ගොඩනැගීමට පිළිකුලකි. විවිධ ආකාරවලින්, ඔවුන් සෑම කෙනෙකුම තම විරුද්ධත්වය "සතුරා" බවට පත් කළ අතර, මාධ්‍ය, රාජ්‍ය නොවන සංවිධාන සහ තමන්ගේම හැර වෙනත් ඕනෑම ඡන්ද කොට්ඨාසයක් අවහිර කළේය.

ජාතීන්, විශේෂයෙන්ම ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී රටවල් බොහෝ එන්ජින් මත ක්‍රියාත්මක වේ - පක්‍ෂයේ හෝ බලයේ සිටින පුද්ගලයන්ගේ තනි ඒකලිතික එකක් පමණක් නොවේ. නව දිල්ලිය කලාපීය නායකයෙකු ලෙස එහි භූමිකාව රචනය කරන බැවින්, ඉන්දියානු ප්‍රතිපත්තියට අසල්වැසි ප්‍රදේශයේ වෙනස්කම්වල බලපෑම අධ්‍යයනය කිරීම පමණක් නොව, අසල්වැසියන් ඉන්දියාව දෙස බලා සිටින කැඩපත දෙස බැලීමට ද රජය ඥානවන්ත වනු ඇත. රට තුළ අනාගත අභියෝග.

ta.title: மியான்மர், நேபாளம், ஆப்கானிஸ்தான், பாகிஸ்தான் மற்றும் இலங்கை ஆகிய நாடுகளில் தேர்தல் அல்லாத மாற்றங்களை நோக்கிய இந்தியாவின் பதிலில், மோடி அரசாங்கத்தின் முந்தைய “மஸ்குலர் ஃபாரின் பாலிசியில்” இருந்து தற்போதைய சமரச அண்டைக் கொள்கைக்கு மாறுவது கவனிக்கத்தக்கது.
body_lines: 2016 ஆம் ஆண்டின் பிற்பகுதியில், இந்திய அரசாங்கத்தின் மூத்த அதிகாரி ஒருவர், அண்டை நாட்டின் தூதுவருடனான சந்திப்பில், நரேந்திர மோடி அரசாங்கத்தின் புதிய "மஸ்குலர் வெளியுறவுக் கொள்கையை" மீறினால், நாடு எதிர்கொள்ளும் விளைவுகளை விவரித்தார். அந்த நேரத்தில், அரசாங்கம் பயங்கரவாதத் தாக்குதல்கள் குறித்து பாகிஸ்தானை நோட்டீஸ் கொடுத்தது, மேலும் வெளியுறவுச் செயலர் அளவிலான பேச்சுவார்த்தைகளை ரத்து செய்தது; இலங்கையில், சீனாவுடன் இணக்கமாக இருப்பதாகக் கருதப்பட்ட ராஜபக்சே அரசாங்கம், எதிர்க்கட்சிகளின் பேச்சுக்களை எளிதாக்குவதில் இந்திய உளவுத்துறையின் பங்கு இருப்பதாக சில அறிக்கைகளுடன் வாக்களிக்கப்பட்டது; மற்றும் நேபாளத்தில் பிரதமர் கே.பி. ஷர்மா ஒலியின் கூட்டணி அரசாங்கம் கவிழ்ந்தது, புது தில்லியில் இருந்து இதே போன்ற ஒரு சலசலப்பு ஏற்பட்டது.

எந்தவொரு ஒத்துழையாமை தெற்காசிய அண்டை நாடுகளையும் வெற்றிகொள்ள புது தில்லி அதன் அனைத்து நெம்புகோல்களையும் பயன்படுத்தும் என்பது அதிகாரி தெரிவித்த செய்தி. இருப்பினும், கடினமான செய்திகள் காலப்போக்கில் அதிக ஆதரவைக் காணவில்லை. மோடி அரசாங்கத்தின் இரண்டாவது பதவிக்காலத்தில், அது அண்டை நாடுகளில் மிகவும் ஒருமித்த, இணக்கமான கொள்கையுடன் சமாதானத்தை ஏற்படுத்தியது - உயர்மட்டப் பயணங்கள் மூலம் ஒவ்வொரு நாட்டுடனும் (பாகிஸ்தானைக் கழித்தல்) கண்ணுக்குத் தெரியக்கூடிய வகையில் உறவுகளை மேம்படுத்துதல், வளர்ச்சி உதவி மற்றும் கடன் வரிகளை விரிவுபடுத்துதல் மற்றும் செயல்படுத்துதல் மென்மையான சக்தி இராஜதந்திரத்தின் அவசரம்.

மியான்மர், நேபாளம், ஆப்கானிஸ்தான், பாகிஸ்தான் மற்றும் இலங்கை ஆகிய ஐந்து அண்டை ஜனநாயக நாடுகள் மேல் தேர்தல் அல்லாத மாற்றங்களைச் செய்த பின்னர், 2016 இல் நடந்த நிகழ்வுகளுக்கு இந்தியாவின் பிரதிபலிப்புக்கு இடையேயான வேறுபாடு அப்பட்டமாக உள்ளது. முதல் வித்தியாசம் என்னவென்றால், புது தில்லி அதன் அண்டைத் தலைநகரங்களில் தங்கள் அரசியல் செயல்முறைகளில் தலையிட முயற்சித்ததற்காகப் பொறுப்பேற்கவில்லை. இரண்டாவதாக, சவுத் பிளாக் பிராந்தியத்திற்கு அதன் ஒரே மாதிரியான தசை "ஒரு அளவு அனைவருக்கும் பொருந்தும்" அணுகுமுறையை கைவிட்டது.

மியான்மரில், மோடி அரசாங்கம் தொடர்ந்து ஈடுபட்டு, ஆங் சான் சூகியின் ஜனநாயகத்திற்கான தேசிய லீக் தலைமையிலான அரசாங்கத்தை கவிழ்த்த இராணுவ ஆட்சிக்குழுவுடன் உறவுகளை வலுப்படுத்தியது, ஆப்கானிஸ்தானில் அது தலிபான்களுடன் உறவுகளை துண்டித்தது. அஷ்ரப் கனி நாட்டை விட்டு வெளியேறினார். நேபாளத்தில் சுப்ரீம் கோர்ட் பதவி நீக்கம் செய்யப்பட்ட பிறகு பிரதமர் ஷேர் பகதூர் டியூபா பதவிக்கு வந்த கே.பி. ஒலி, மற்றும் இலங்கை ஆகிய நாடுகளின் மக்கள் எதிர்ப்புக்களால் பிரதமர் மகிந்த ராஜபக்சவை ராஜினாமா செய்ய நிர்ப்பந்தித்தது மற்றும் ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்சவை போட்டியாளரும் எதிர்கட்சித் தலைவருமான ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்கவை புதிய பிரதமராக நியமிக்க, புதுடில்லி பெரும்பாலும் இந்த செயல்முறைகளுக்கு ஆதரவாக இருந்து வருகிறது. அதேசமயம், பாகிஸ்தானில், இம்ரான் கான் நம்பிக்கை வாக்கெடுப்பில் தோல்வியடைந்ததை அடுத்து, பிரதமர் ஷேபாஸ் ஷெரீப்பின் பதவியேற்பை அது புறக்கணித்தது.

கற்றுக்கொண்டதாகத் தோன்றும் இரண்டாவது பாடம் என்னவென்றால், புது தில்லியின் செய்தி இப்போது அதிகாரத்தில் இருப்பவர்களைக் காட்டிலும் அக்கம்பக்கத்தில் உள்ளவர்களை மையமாகக் கொண்டுள்ளது. உதாரணமாக, ஆப்கானிஸ்தானில், மோடி அரசாங்கம், இஸ்லாமாபாத் அல்லது காபூலுடன் இராஜதந்திர நிச்சயதார்த்தம் இல்லாத போதிலும், ஆப்கானிஸ்தான் மக்களுக்காக 50,000 மெட்ரிக் டன் கோதுமையை அனுப்ப முடியும் என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்த, பாகிஸ்தான் அதிகாரிகளுடன் பல மாதங்கள் கவனமாகப் பேச்சுவார்த்தை நடத்தியது. இலங்கையில், வெளிவிவகார அமைச்சின் அறிக்கை, "ஜனநாயக செயல்முறைகள் மூலம் வெளிப்படுத்தப்படும் இலங்கை மக்களின் நலன்களால் இந்தியா எப்போதும் வழிநடத்தப்படும்", இது மக்களுக்கும் பிராந்தியத்தில் உள்ள ஜனநாயக செயல்முறைகளுக்கும் நுட்பமான சுருதியாகும். மூன்றாவது பாடம், அக்கம் பக்கத்திலுள்ள உள்நாட்டுப் பிரச்சினைகளில் சொல்லாடல்களைக் குறைப்பது - கடந்த ஆண்டு வங்காளதேசத்தின் இந்து சிறுபான்மையினருக்கு எதிரான துர்கா பூஜை வன்முறைக்கு அரசாங்கத்தின் பொது எதிர்வினை, குடியுரிமை (திருத்தம்) சட்டத்திற்கு முன்னோக்கிச் செல்லும் போது செய்தி அனுப்புவதை விட மிகவும் நுணுக்கமாக இருந்தது. 2019. CAA க்கான விதிகளை வகுப்பதில் தொடர்ந்து தாமதம் ஏற்படுவது, கோவிட்-19 தொற்றுநோயைக் காட்டிலும் டாக்காவுடனான உறவுகளைப் பற்றிய அக்கறையுடன் தொடர்புடையது என்று சிலர் பரிந்துரைத்துள்ளனர்.

எவ்வாறாயினும், அண்டை நாடுகளில் ஏற்பட்டுள்ள ஆட்சி மாற்றங்களிலிருந்து புது தில்லி கற்றுக்கொள்ள வேண்டிய மற்ற பாடங்கள் உள்ளன, அவற்றில் சில இந்திய சூழலுக்கும் பொருந்தும். இது எல்லாவற்றிற்கும் மேலாக, இந்தியப் பெருங்கடலில் அமைக்கப்பட்ட இந்தியத் துணைக் கண்டம், இங்கு நடப்பது இந்தியாவைத் தொடாமல் விட்டுவிட முடியாது. எனவே, ஒரு மௌனமான அல்லது "நடுநிலை" நிலைப்பாடு, உக்ரைன் மீதான ரஷ்யாவின் ஆக்கிரமிப்பு அல்லது ஹாங்காங் அல்லது தென் சீனக் கடல் பகுதியில் சீனாவின் நகர்வுகள் போன்றவற்றில் ஏற்பட்ட மாற்றங்களுக்கு மோடி அரசாங்கத்தின் பதிலைக் குறிக்க முடியாது. ஒரு அண்டை நாட்டில் உருவாகும் இயக்கங்கள் பெரும்பாலும் மற்றொரு நாட்டில் பிரதிபலிக்கப்படுவதால், உதவி மற்றும் கடன்கள் அல்லது அகதிகளின் சாத்தியமான வருகை ஆகியவற்றின் அடிப்படையில், நெருக்கடியில் உள்ள ஒவ்வொரு தெற்காசிய நாடுகளின் நேரடி தாக்கத்தை இந்தியா எதிர்கொள்கிறது. எனவே, அவர்கள் இன்னும் உன்னிப்பாக கவனிக்கப்பட வேண்டும்.

கற்க வேண்டிய முதல் பாடம் என்னவென்றால், ஜனரஞ்சகவாதம் நீண்ட காலத்திற்கு பலனளிக்காது. அதி-தேசியவாதம், மதப் பெரும்பான்மைவாதம் மற்றும் கடுமையான எலிட்டிசம் ஆகியவற்றின் வலிமையான கலவையானது மகிந்த ராஜபக்ச, கே.பி. ஒலி, மற்றும் இம்ரான் கான் ஆட்சிக்கு (ஊழல், வம்ச ஆட்சிகளுக்கு மாற்றாக அவர்கள் உறுதியளிப்பதால்), அது அவர்களை அங்கேயே வைத்திருக்க வேண்டிய அவசியமில்லை. எந்த அரசாங்கமும் தேர்தல் வெற்றி மற்றும் ஆட்சிக்கான ஆணையை ஒரு நாட்டை ஆள்வதற்கான கார்டே பிளான்ச் உடன் இணைப்பது தவறு.

இரண்டாவதாக, ஒரு தலைவரின் புகழ் ஒன்று அல்லது பல காரணங்களால் கடுமையாகவும் திடீரெனவும் குறையலாம்: கே.பி. ஓலி 2017 இல் மகத்தான வெற்றியைப் பெற்றார், அங்கு அவரது இடது கூட்டணி இரு அவைகளிலும் பெரும்பான்மையைப் பெற்றது, மேலும் ஏழு மாகாணங்களில் ஆறில் அரசாங்கங்களை அமைத்தது; இம்ரான் கான் 2018 தேர்தல்களில் அவர் போட்டியிட்ட ஐந்து தேசிய சட்டமன்ற இடங்களிலும் வெற்றி பெற்றார், மேலும் அவரது கட்சி பெரும்பான்மை இடங்களை வெல்லவில்லை என்றாலும், அது மக்கள் வாக்குகளைப் பெற்றது; மற்றும் ராஜபக்சே தலைமையிலான ஸ்ரீலங்கா மக்கள் கட்சி (SLPP) ஆளும் கூட்டணி 2020 இல் 225 நாடாளுமன்ற இடங்களில் 150 இடங்களை வென்றது. இந்த மக்கள் ஆணையை ஒரு சில ஆண்டுகளில் ஒதுக்கித் தள்ள முடியும் என்பது, குறிப்பாக ஜனநாயகத்தில் எதுவும் நிரந்தரமில்லை என்பதை நினைவூட்டுகிறது.

நேபாளம், பாக்கிஸ்தான் மற்றும் இலங்கை சந்தித்த நெருக்கடிகளின் போது, ​​அத்துமீறலில் இறங்கிய தலைவர்கள், அவர்கள் மாற்றிய தலைவர்களைப் போன்ற அரசியல் திறமையோ அல்லது பேச்சுத்திறமையோ இல்லாதவர்களாக இருந்திருக்கலாம், ஆனால் அவர்கள் உள்நாட்டிலும் சர்வதேச அளவிலும் ஏற்றுக்கொள்ளப்பட்டனர் என்பதும் தெளிவாகிறது. அவர்கள் பக்கம் அனுபவமும் கல்வியும் இருந்தது. திரு. டியூபா ஐந்தாவது முறையாக பிரதமரானார், உதாரணமாக, திரு. ஷெரீப் பிரதமராகும் முன் மூன்று முறை பாகிஸ்தானின் பஞ்சாபின் முதல்வராக நீண்ட காலம் பதவி வகித்தவர், மேலும் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்கே ஆறாவது முறையாக பிரதமராக நியமிக்கப்பட்டார்.

அடுத்த பாடம், அமெரிக்க அதிபர் ஜார்ஜ் எச்.டபிள்யூ. புஷ் 1992 இல் ஈராக் போர் மற்றும் தேசபக்திக்கான அவரது சுருதி இருந்தபோதிலும், "இது பொருளாதாரம், முட்டாள்" என்று பெரிய கோஷமாக இருந்த தேர்தலில் பில் கிளிண்டன் அவரை தோற்கடித்ததால் கற்றுக்கொண்டார். பாக்கிஸ்தான், நேபாளம் மற்றும் இலங்கையில், ஜனரஞ்சகவாதிகளின் தோல்வி எதிர்க்கட்சிகளின் கைகளால் அதிகம் அல்ல, மாறாக வளர்ச்சி மந்தநிலை, வேலை வாய்ப்புகள் மற்றும் அதிகரித்து வரும் பணவீக்கம் ஆகியவற்றால் வந்துள்ளது. 2019 டிசம்பரில் இந்தியா ஏற்கனவே ஆறு காலாண்டு நேரான இழப்புகளைக் கண்டிருந்தது, மேலும் கோவிட்-19 முதன்முதலில் அறிவிக்கப்பட்டபோது, ​​பெரும்பாலான சுற்றுப்புறங்களும் தத்தளித்தன.

தொடர்ந்து வந்த ஆண்டுகளில், கோவிட்-19 தொற்றுநோய் லாக்டவுன்களை அமல்படுத்தியது, அதன் விளைவாக உலகப் பொருளாதாரத்தில் ஏற்பட்ட மந்தநிலை, பிராந்தியத்தில் மொத்த உள்நாட்டு உற்பத்தியின் புள்ளிவிவரங்கள் வீழ்ச்சியடையச் செய்தது. மிக சமீபத்தில், உக்ரைன் மீதான ரஷ்யாவின் படையெடுப்பு மற்றும் மேற்கு நாடுகளின் பொருளாதாரத் தடைகள் உணவு மற்றும் எரிபொருள் விலைகளை உயர்த்தியுள்ளன. அத்தகைய சூழ்நிலையில், இந்த மூன்று நாடுகளிலும் அதிகார மாற்றம் இராணுவம், நீதிமன்றங்கள் அல்லது தெருப் போராட்டங்களிலிருந்து ஒரு சிறிய உந்துதலை மட்டுமே எடுத்தது. அண்டை நாடுகளில் மாற்றத்தை கொண்டு வந்த பொருளாதார தவறான நிர்வாகத்தின் காரணங்களை புது தில்லி ஆய்வு செய்வது மட்டுமல்லாமல், அப்பகுதியில் நேரடி செல்வாக்கை தேடும் போது உலக சக்திகளால் சுரண்டப்படக்கூடிய சிறிய அண்டை நாடுகளில் ஏற்படும் புதிய பாதிப்புகளின் தாக்கத்தையும் ஆய்வு செய்ய வேண்டும்.

பிராந்தியம் எதிர்கொள்ளும் பொதுவான சவால்களைக் கருத்தில் கொண்டு, பல்துறை தொழில்நுட்ப மற்றும் பொருளாதார ஒத்துழைப்புக்கான வங்காள விரிகுடா முன்முயற்சி (BIMSTEC) மற்றும் வங்காளதேசம், பூட்டான், இந்தியா, நேபாளம் (BBIN) முன்முயற்சி போன்ற பிராந்திய குழுக்களை உற்சாகப்படுத்த புது தில்லி புதிய வழிகளைக் கண்டறிய வேண்டும். மற்றும் SAARC ஐ மறுபரிசீலனை செய்வதற்கும் கூட, சுற்றுலா மற்றும் ஏற்றுமதியை புதுப்பித்தல், வெளிநாட்டில் உள்ள தெற்காசிய வெளிநாட்டு தொழிலாளர்களை ஆதரித்தல் மற்றும் தெற்காசியப் பொருளாதாரத்தின் மீதான பணவீக்க தாக்கங்களைத் தணிக்க பொதுவான உணவு மற்றும் எரிபொருள் இருப்புக்களை உருவாக்குதல் ஆகியவற்றுக்கான பகிரப்பட்ட அணுகுமுறைகளைப் பற்றி விவாதிக்க வேண்டும்.

இறுதியாக, அண்டை ஜனநாயக நாடுகளில் உள்ள "ஆல்ஃபா தலைவர்களை" வீழ்த்தும் அரசியல் கலாச்சாரத்தின் பாடங்களிலிருந்து மோடி அரசாங்கம் கற்றுக்கொள்ள வேண்டும். ஒவ்வொரு அரசாங்கத்திலும் உள்ள பொதுவான இழைகளில் ஒன்று (ராஜபக்ஷ, ஒலி மற்றும் கான்) ஒருமித்த கருத்தை உருவாக்குவதற்கான வெறுப்பு. பல்வேறு வழிகளில், அவர்கள் ஒவ்வொருவரும் தங்கள் எதிர்ப்பை "எதிரி"யாக மாற்றினர், மேலும் ஊடகங்கள், அரசு சாரா நிறுவனங்கள் மற்றும் தங்களுடையதைத் தவிர வேறு எந்த வாக்களிக்கும் தொகுதிகளையும் முடக்கினர்.

நாடுகள், குறிப்பாக ஜனநாயகங்கள் பல இயந்திரங்களில் இயங்குகின்றன - கட்சி அல்லது அதிகாரத்தில் உள்ளவர்களின் ஒற்றை ஒற்றைக்கல் மட்டும் அல்ல. புது தில்லி ஒரு பிராந்தியத் தலைவராக தனது பங்கை எழுதுகையில், இந்தியக் கொள்கையில் அண்டை நாடுகளில் ஏற்படும் மாற்றங்களின் தாக்கத்தை ஆய்வு செய்வது மட்டுமல்லாமல், அண்டை நாடுகள் இந்தியாவைப் பற்றி நன்றாகப் புரிந்துகொள்வதற்கு கண்ணாடியைப் பார்ப்பதும் புத்திசாலித்தனமாக இருக்கும். நாட்டின் எதிர்கால சவால்கள்.

en.title: The Shift from  the Modi Govt’s Earlier “Muscular Foreign Policy” to the Current Conciliatory Neighbourhood  Policy is Noticeable in  India’s Response Towards Non-electoral Changes at the top in Myanmar, Nepal, Afghanistan, Pakistan and Sri Lanka.
body_lines: In late 2016, a senior Indian government official, in a meeting with the envoy of a neighbouring country, spelt out the consequences that the country would face if it crossed the Narendra Modi government’s newly “muscular foreign policy”. At the time, the Government had put Pakistan on notice on terror attacks, and cancelled Foreign Secretary-level talks; in Sri Lanka, the Rajapaksa government, that had been seen cozying up to China was voted out, with some reports that Indian intelligence played a role in facilitating opposition talks; and in Nepal, Prime Minister K.P. Sharma Oli’s coalition government had fallen apart, with a similar nudge reported from New Delhi.

The message the official conveyed was that New Delhi would use all its levers to prevail over any uncooperative South Asian neighbour. However, the tough messaging did not find much favour over time. By the Modi government’s second term, it had made peace with a much more consensual, conciliatory policy in the neighbourhood — visibly improving ties with each country (minus Pakistan) through high-level visits, extending development aid and lines of credit, and enabling a rush of soft power diplomacy.

The contrast between India’s response to the events in 2016 to the present is stark, after five neighbouring democracies underwent non-electoral changes at the top, namely Myanmar, Nepal, Afghanistan, Pakistan and Sri Lanka. The first difference is that New Delhi has not been held responsible in any of its neighbouring capitals for attempting to interfere in their political processes. Second, South Block has abandoned its uniformly muscular “one size fits all” approach to the region.

While in Myanmar, the Modi government continued engagement and even strengthened ties with the military junta that overthrew the government led by Aung San Suu Kyi’s National League for Democracy, in Afghanistan it severed ties with the Taliban that took power in Kabul by force after Afghan President Ashraf Ghani left the country. In Nepal where Prime Minister Sher Bahadur Deuba came to power after the Supreme Court dismissed K.P. Oli, and Sri Lanka, where public protests forced Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa to resign and President Gotabaya Rajapaksa to appoint rival and Opposition leader Ranil Wickremesinghe as the new Prime Minister, New Delhi has been largely supportive of the processes. Whereas in Pakistan, it has virtually ignored the swearing-in of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif after Imran Khan lost the vote of confidence.

A second lesson that seems to have been learnt is that New Delhi’s messaging now is focused on people in the neighbourhood rather than just those in power. In Afghanistan, for example, the Modi government spent months in careful negotiation with Pakistani officials to ensure it could send 50,000 MT of wheat meant for the Afghan people, despite the fact that it has no diplomatic engagement with either Islamabad or Kabul otherwise. In Sri Lanka, a Ministry of External Affairs statement said that India would “always be guided by the best interests of the people of Sri Lanka expressed through democratic processes”, a subtle pitch both to the people and to democratic processes in the region. A third lesson is perhaps the toning down of rhetoric on domestic issues in the neighbourhood — the Government’s public reaction to Durga Pooja violence against Bangladesh’s Hindu minority last year was much more nuanced than its messaging during the push ahead for the Citizenship (Amendment) Act in 2019. Some have even suggested that the continued delay in framing rules for the CAA since then has more to do with concern for ties with Dhaka than with the COVID-19 pandemic.

There are, however, other lessons that New Delhi must learn from the regime changes in the neighbourhood, and some of them apply to the Indian context as well. This is after all, the Indian subcontinent, set in the Indian Ocean, and what happens here cannot leave India untouched. Therefore, a silent or “neutral” position cannot mark the Modi government’s response to the changes in the way it has with Russia’s invasion of Ukraine or China’s moves in Hong Kong or the South China Sea region. India faces the direct impact of almost every South Asian country in crisis, in terms of the need for aid and loans or a possible influx of refugees, as movements that develop in one neighbouring country are often mirrored in another. Therefore, they must be watched more closely.

The first lesson to be learnt is that populism does not pay in the long run. While the potent combination of hyper-nationalism, religious majoritarianism, and a strident anti-elitism can bring “men of the masses” such as Mahinda Rajapaksa, K.P. Oli, and Imran Khan to power (as they promise an alternative to corrupt, dynasti regimes), it does not necessarily keep them there. It is a mistake for any government to conflate an electoral win and a mandate for governance with a carte blanche for ruling a country.

The second is that the popularity of a leader can decline sharply and suddenly for one or a combination of reasons: K.P. Oli won a landslide victory in 2017 where his Left Alliance secured majorities in both houses, and formed governments in six of seven provinces; Imran Khan won all five National Assembly seats he fought in the 2018 elections, and while his party did not win a majority of seats, it won the popular vote; and the Rajapaksa-led Sri Lanka People’s Party (SLPP) ruling coalition won 150 of 225 parliamentary seats in 2020. That these popular mandates could be cast aside in just a few years is a stark reminder that nothing is forever, especially in a democracy.

It is also clear that during the crises that Nepal, Pakistan and Sri Lanka faced, the leaders who stepped into the breach may not have had the same political prowess or oratory presence as the leaders they replaced, but were acceptable both domestically and internationally because they had experience and education on their side. Mr. Deuba became Prime Minister for the fifth time, for example, Mr. Sharif had the longest tenure as Chief Minister of Pakistan’s Punjab in three terms before he became Prime Minister, and Ranil Wickremesinghe was appointed Prime Minister for the sixth time.

The next lesson is one that United States President George H.W. Bush learned in 1992 despite the Iraq war and his pitch to patriotism, as Bill Clinton defeated him in an election where the big slogan was “It’s the economy, stupid”. In Pakistan, Nepal and Sri Lanka, the defeat of populists has come not so much at the hand of the Opposition parties, but by the slowdown in growth, jobs and rising inflation. India had already seen six successive quarters of straight losses in December 2019, and most of the neighbourhood was floundering as well, when COVID-19 was first reported.

In the years that followed, the COVID-19 pandemic enforced lockdowns, and the resultant slowdown in the global economy made GDP figures in the region plummet. More recently, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and sanctions by the West have made food and fuel prices soar. In such a situation, the change in power in these three countries took only a small push, from the military, the courts, or from street protests. New Delhi must not only study the causes of the economic mismanagement that brought change in the neighbourhood but must also survey the impact of new vulnerabilities on smaller neighbouring countries that could be exploited by global powers as they seek a more direct influence in the region.

Given the common challenges the region faces, New Delhi must find newer ways to energise regional groupings such as the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) and the Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal (BBIN) Initiative, and even to reconsider SAARC, in order to discuss shared approaches to reviving tourism and exports, supporting South Asian expatriate labour abroad, and building common pools of food and fuel stocks to soften inflationary blows on the South Asian economy.

Finally, the Modi government must learn from the lessons in political culture that let down the “alpha leaders” in neighbouring democracies. One of the common threads in each of the governments (Rajapaksa, Oli and Khan) was an abhorrence for consensus building. In various ways, each of them turned their opposition into “the enemy”, and froze out the media, non-governmental organisations, and any voting constituency other than their own.

Nations, especially democracies run on many engines — not just the single monolithic one of the party or people in power. As New Delhi essays its role as a regional leader, the Government would be wise to not only study the impact of changes in the neighbourhood on Indian policy but also to look into the mirror the neighbours hold up to India, for a better understanding of its future challenges within the country.
